name: Premium Rewards Credit Card
effective_cost: Moderate: $200 in travel credits offset roughly two-thirds of the fee if you use both

verdict.text: Keep if the $200 credits offset the fee: solid no-frills 2x rate

earn_note: No caps on the 2x categories or the 1.5x base, a genuinely simple structure for a $95 card. Enrolled BofA Rewards members get a 10–75% bonus on top of these rates depending on combined deposit/investment balance.

redemption_options.method: Deposit to a Bank of America checking or savings account
Statement credit against travel purchases, or gift cards

points.note: Redemption tops out around 1¢/point regardless of method. There's no transfer-partner program behind these points, so the ceiling is lower than a transferable-currency card at this price point.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners: points redeem directly for cash, travel statement credit, or gift cards at a flat rate.

credits.name: Airline Incidental Statement Credit
credits.subtitle: auto-applies · seat upgrades, bags, in-flight, lounge fees
credits.description: Up to $100 annually in statement credits for qualifying airline incidental purchases: seat upgrades, baggage fees, in-flight services, and airline lounge day-pass fees.
tips: Covers one-off airline lounge day passes, but this card doesn't include a lounge membership the way Premium Rewards Elite does.

credits.name: Global Entry / TSA PreCheck Credit
credits.subtitle: $100 · every 4 years
credits.description: Statement credit covering the application fee (up to $100) for Global Entry or TSA PreCheck, once every 4 years, when the fee is charged to this card. Shown here amortized to an annual figure.
tips: Apply for Global Entry rather than just TSA PreCheck; it includes TSA PreCheck and still qualifies for this credit.

insurance.detail: Covered: exact dollar limits are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Covered: exact dollar limits are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Covered: exact dollar limits are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Covered: exact dollar limits are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Covered: exact dollar limits are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Covered: exact terms are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Covered (Purchase Security): exact limits are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Covered (Warranty Manager Service): exact limits are in the Guide to Benefits, not public marketing
Not responsible for fraudulent transactions once reported

protection_note: Bank of America confirms this card includes a real travel-insurance and purchase-protection suite (trip delay/cancellation/interruption, baggage delay, lost luggage, emergency evacuation, Return Protection, and Purchase Security), but doesn't publish exact dollar limits or day thresholds on the product page the way Amex/Chase do. Check your Guide to Benefits after approval for the real numbers.
rental_note: Auto Rental Collision Damage Waiver is included, plus 24/7 Roadside Dispatch, but exact coverage terms (primary vs. secondary, dollar limits, excluded vehicles) aren't published publicly.

services.name: 60,000-point welcome bonus
services.detail: A $600 value, after $4,000 in purchases within the first 90 days.

services.name: Visa Signature Luxury Hotel Collection
services.detail: Room upgrades, late checkout, and other perks when booking through the Visa Signature Luxury Hotel Collection.

services.name: 24/7 concierge
services.detail: Visa Signature concierge service for travel planning, dining reservations, and event tickets.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: $0 on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: BofA Rewards bonus
services.detail: 10% (Member), 25% (Preferred Plus), 50% (Preferred Honors), or 75% (Premier) bonus on points earned, based on combined Bank of America/Merrill balance.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns points on their spend (into your account)
Covered by the same purchase and travel protections

additional_cards.note: No cost to add: a simple way to pool a partner's or family member's travel/dining spend into the 2x categories.
